User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account in the SquadForge application, including profile information and notification preferences.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

fullName.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for login and communication.

phone.description: The optional phone number of the user.

avatarUrl.description: The URL to the user's optional profile avatar image.

notificationsEnabled.description: A flag indicating if the user has globally enabled push notifications.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

Team.title: Team
Team.description: Represents a sports team or group within the SquadForge application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Team entity.

sport.description: The sport associated with the team (e.g., 'Basketball', 'Soccer').

teamLogoUrl.description: The URL to the team's optional logo image.

teamCode.description: A unique 6-character alphanumeric code for joining the team.

createdBy.description: Reference to the User who created this team. (Relationship: User 1:N Team)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the team was created.

TeamMembership.title: TeamMembership
TeamMembership.description: Represents a user's membership within a specific team, including their role and team-specific details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TeamMembership entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who is a member. (Relationship: User 1:N TeamMembership)

teamId.description: Reference to the Team this membership belongs to. (Relationship: Team 1:N TeamMembership)

role.description: The role of the user within this specific team ('Admin' or 'Member').

position.description: The user's playing position within the team (optional, editable by admins).

jerseyNumber.description: The user's jersey number within the team (optional, editable by admins).

joinedAt.description: Timestamp when the user joined this team.

Post.title: Post
Post.description: Represents a post in a team's activity feed, which can be created by a user or automatically by the system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Post entity.

teamId.description: Reference to the Team this post belongs to. (Relationship: Team 1:N Post)

authorId.description: Reference to the User who authored the post. For system posts, this could be null or a system user ID. (Relationship: User 1:N Post)

type.description: The type of post, indicating if it's a user-generated post or an automated system notification (e.g., 'userPost', 'eventCreated', 'eventUpdated', 'memberJoined').

content.description: The text content of the post.

imageUrl.description: The URL of an optional single image attached to the post.

linkedEventId.description: Optional reference to an Event if this post is related to an event. (Relationship: Event 1:N Post)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the post was created.

Comment.title: Comment
Comment.description: Represents a comment made on a Post in the team's activity feed.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Comment entity.

postId.description: Reference to the Post this comment belongs to. (Relationship: Post 1:N Comment)

authorId.description: Reference to the User who authored the comment. (Relationship: User 1:N Comment)

content.description: The text content of the comment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the comment was created.

Event.title: Event
Event.description: Represents a scheduled event for a team, created by an Admin.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Event entity.

teamId.description: Reference to the Team this event belongs to. (Relationship: Team 1:N Event)

title.description: The title or name of the event.

startTime.description: The start time of the event.

endTime.description: The optional end time of the event.

location.description: The location where the event will take place.

description.description: A detailed description of the event.

createdBy.description: Reference to the User (Admin) who created this event. (Relationship: User 1:N Event)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the event was created.

RSVP.title: RSVP
RSVP.description: Records a user's response to an event invitation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RSVP entity.

eventId.description: Reference to the Event this RSVP is for. (Relationship: Event 1:N RSVP)

userId.description: Reference to the User who made the RSVP. (Relationship: User 1:N RSVP)

status.description: The user's RSVP status for the event ('Going', 'Not Going', 'Maybe').

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the RSVP status was last updated.

Availability.title: Availability
Availability.description: Records a team member's self-reported unavailability for a specific date.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Availability entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User reporting unavailability. (Relationship: User 1:N Availability)

teamId.description: Reference to the Team this unavailability applies to. (Relationship: Team 1:N Availability)

date.description: The specific date for which the user is unavailable.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the unavailability was recorded.

File.title: File
File.description: Represents a file uploaded and shared within a team.

id.description: Unique identifier for the File entity.

teamId.description: Reference to the Team this file belongs to. (Relationship: Team 1:N File)

fileUrl.description: The URL where the file is stored.

fileName.description: The original name of the uploaded file.

fileSize.description: The size of the file in bytes.

uploadedBy.description: Reference to the User who uploaded the file. (Relationship: User 1:N File)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the file was uploaded.

GroupChat.title: GroupChat
GroupChat.description: Represents a group chat within a team, allowing multiple members to communicate.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GroupChat entity.

teamId.description: Reference to the Team this chat belongs to. (Relationship: Team 1:N GroupChat)

name.description: The name of the group chat.

createdBy.description: Reference to the User who created this group chat. (Relationship: User 1:N GroupChat)

memberIds.description: An array of User IDs who are members of this group chat. (Relationship: User N:N GroupChat)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the group chat was created.

isDeleted.description: A flag indicating if the chat has been globally deleted by an admin. False by default.

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: Represents a single message or poll within a GroupChat.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatMessage entity.

chatId.description: Reference to the GroupChat this message belongs to. (Relationship: GroupChat 1:N ChatMessage)

authorId.description: Reference to the User who sent this message. (Relationship: User 1:N ChatMessage)

type.description: The type of the chat message ('text' or 'poll').

content.description: The text content of the message (if type is 'text').

pollId.description: Optional reference to a Poll if the message type is 'poll'. (Relationship: Poll 1:1 ChatMessage)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the message was sent.

UserChatHidden.title: UserChatHidden
UserChatHidden.description: Records when a specific user has hidden a group chat from their view, without globally deleting it.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserChatHidden entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who hid the chat. (Relationship: User 1:N UserChatHidden)

chatId.description: Reference to the GroupChat that was hidden. (Relationship: GroupChat 1:N UserChatHidden)

hiddenAt.description: Timestamp when the user hid the chat.

Poll.title: Poll
Poll.description: Represents a poll created within a group chat for team members to vote on.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Poll entity.

chatId.description: Reference to the GroupChat where this poll was created. (Relationship: GroupChat 1:N Poll)

question.description: The question posed in the poll.

options.description: An array of string options for the poll. Minimum 2, maximum 6 options.

createdBy.description: Reference to the User who created this poll. (Relationship: User 1:N Poll)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the poll was created.

isClosed.description: A flag indicating if the poll is closed and no longer accepting votes. False by default.

PollVote.title: PollVote
PollVote.description: Records a single user's vote on a specific poll option.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PollVote entity.

pollId.description: Reference to the Poll this vote is for. (Relationship: Poll 1:N PollVote)

userId.description: Reference to the User who cast this vote. (Relationship: User 1:N PollVote)

selectedOptionIndex.description: The index of the option (from the Poll's 'options' array) that the user voted for.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the vote was cast or last changed.

definition.description: Root collection for user profiles. Each document ID (`userId`) corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID, ensuring path-based ownership. Users can only read/write their own profile.

Subcollection storing records of chats hidden by a specific user. The `userId` in the path ensures that only the owner can access their hidden chat preferences. No additional denormalized fields are required for authorization beyond the path-based ownership.

Root collection for all teams. Each document represents a team. Includes a denormalized 'members' map (`members: { [userId: string]: 'admin' | 'member' }`) for authorization independence, listing all team members and their roles. This map is crucial for quick rule evaluations on team membership without `get()` calls.

Subcollection representing a user's membership within a specific team. The `userId` in the path corresponds to the member's UID. This document contains detailed member information (role, position, jerseyNumber) specific to the team. Access is controlled by the parent 'team' document's 'members' map.
Subcollection for team activity feed posts. Includes denormalized 'team_members' map for authorization independence, inherited from the parent team, allowing rules to check team membership directly.

Subcollection for comments on a specific post. Includes denormalized 'teamId' and 'team_members' map for authorization independence, inherited from the grandparent team, allowing rules to check team membership directly without traversing ancestors.
Subcollection for team events. Includes denormalized 'team_members' map for authorization independence, inherited from the parent team, allowing rules to check team membership directly.

Subcollection for RSVPs to a specific event. Includes denormalized 'teamId' and 'team_members' map for authorization independence, inherited from the grandparent team, allowing rules to check team membership directly. Each RSVP document's ID (`rsvpId`) should be the `userId` for uniqueness and easy lookup.

Subcollection for team members' availability. Includes denormalized 'team_members' map for authorization independence, inherited from the parent team, allowing rules to check team membership directly. Each Availability document's ID (`availabilityId`) should be the `userId` + `date` for uniqueness and easy lookup.

Subcollection for files shared within a team. Includes denormalized 'team_members' map for authorization independence, inherited from the parent team, allowing rules to check team membership directly.

Subcollection for group chats within a team. Includes denormalized 'team_members' map for authorization independence, inherited from the parent team. The 'memberIds' array is replaced by a 'chat_members' map (`chat_members: { [userId: string]: boolean }`) for efficient rule evaluation without `get()` calls, enabling QAPs for chat access.

Subcollection for messages within a group chat. Includes denormalized 'teamId', 'team_members' map, and 'chat_members' map for authorization independence, inherited from the grandparent team and parent chat, allowing rules to check both team and chat membership directly.

Subcollection for polls created within a group chat. Includes denormalized 'teamId', 'team_members' map, and 'chat_members' map for authorization independence, inherited from the grandparent team and parent chat, allowing rules to check both team and chat membership directly.

Subcollection for votes on a specific poll. Includes denormalized 'teamId', 'team_members' map, 'chatId', 'chat_members' map, and 'poll_isClosed' boolean for authorization independence, inherited from ancestor documents, allowing rules to check membership and poll status directly. Each PollVote document's ID (`voteId`) should be the `userId` for uniqueness and easy lookup per poll.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for SquadForge prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The primary goal is **Authorization Independence**, which is achieved through comprehensive denormalization of authorization contexts, and **Structural Segregation** to simplify security rules.

**Addressing the user's reported error:** The error `FirestorePermissionError: Missing or insufficient permissions: The following request was denied by Firestore Security Rules: { ..., "method": "list", "path": "/databases/(default)/documents/leagues/global/invites" }` indicates a permission issue on a path `/leagues/global/invites`. However, the provided application requirements and entity definitions do not contain any mention of 'leagues' or 'invites'. Therefore, this structure is designed exclusively based on the provided 'Application Data Entities & Auth Info' and 'App requirements'. It is assumed that the reported error path is either from a legacy feature, an unrelated part of the application, or an entity not included in this design scope.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) via Denormalization:**
To eliminate `get()` calls in security rules and enable atomic operations, crucial authorization data is denormalized directly into the documents that require it for access control:

1.  **Team-Level Authorization:** Each `Team` document (`/teams/{teamId}`) will store a `members` map (e.g., `{"uid1": "admin", "uid2": "member"}`). This map is then denormalized into *all documents within that team's subcollections* (Posts, Comments, Events, RSVPs, Availability, Files, GroupChats, ChatMessages, Polls, PollVotes) as `team_members`. This means a rule for a Post (`/teams/{teamId}/posts/{postId}`) can check `request.auth.uid in resource.data.team_members` directly, without needing to `get()` the parent `Team` document.

2.  **GroupChat-Level Authorization:** Similarly, each `GroupChat` document (`/teams/{teamId}/groupChats/{chatId}`) will store a `chat_members` map (e.g., `{"uid1": true, "uid2": true}`). This map is denormalized into all documents within that chat's subcollections (ChatMessages, Polls, PollVotes) as `chat_members`.

3.  **Poll Status Denormalization:** For `PollVote` documents, the `poll_isClosed` status from the parent `Poll` document is denormalized as `poll_isClosed` to allow rules to enforce voting restrictions without a `get()` call.

4.  **Implicit `teamId` and `chatId`:** For deeply nested subcollections like Comments, RSVPs, ChatMessages, Polls, and PollVotes, the `teamId` and `chatId` (where applicable) are explicitly added to the document's fields. This ensures consistent queryability and local context for rules, without traversing parent paths.

**Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):**

*   **User-Specific Data:** Private data for individual users, such as `User` profiles and `UserChatHidden` preferences, are stored under a user's dedicated path (`/users/{userId}/...`). This ensures that only the authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`) can access their own data, simplifying rules considerably.
*   **Team-Specific Data:** All data belonging to a team (Posts, Events, Files, Chats, etc.) are organized as subcollections under `/teams/{teamId}/...`. This clearly segments team data and allows security rules to enforce team-level membership and role-based access for all documents within a given team's hierarchy.
*   **Team Membership:** `TeamMembership` documents, which define a user's role and details within a specific team, are stored in a subcollection (`/teams/{teamId}/members/{userId}`). This centralizes roster data and allows efficient lookups of a user's role within a team.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

By denormalizing `team_members` and `chat_members` maps into documents, the structure inherently supports Querying All Possibilities (QAPs). For example, to list posts for a given team, a client can query `/teams/{teamId}/posts` with a filter like `where('team_members.' + auth.uid, 'exists', true)` (or `== 'member'` / `'admin'`). This ensures that only documents accessible to the authenticated user are returned by the query, preventing security rules from acting as filters post-query, which is inefficient and insecure. The `list` operation for user-specific data (e.g., hidden chats) directly uses `request.auth.uid` in the path, adhering to QAP principles.

This robust design ensures that security rules are simple, explicit, and highly performant, directly aligning with the core design principles.
